rationale: Title X follows Heart Rhythm's standard comparative-study format with a clean head noun phrase, explicit comparator (PFA vs RFA), study design label, and population, while Title Y is fragmented by a colon-separated list of sub-topics that obscures the primary comparison and reads more like a conference abstract header.
loser_weakness: Title Y lacks a stated comparator, buries the core PFA-vs-RFA comparison, and its comma-separated sub-topic list (clinical predictors, renal safety, rhythm outcomes) diffuses focus and departs from Heart Rhythm's concise, single-question titling style.
